The key point here is that exclusive breastfeeding leads to characteristic changes in stool appearance—typically resulting in greenish stools rather than golden-colored ones. Additionally, there is no evidence linking hemolysis due to Vitamin K deficiency specifically to exclusive breastfeeding; instead, vitamin K prophylaxis is routinely recommended shortly after birth regardless of feeding method to prevent hemorrhagic disease of the newborn.
